industry: GitHub Account Reinstatement — the domain operating system for GitHub (and analogous platform) account suspension, restriction, appeal, and recovery. Covers: (a) GitHub official policy architecture (TOS / Acceptable Use Policies / Trust & Safety enforcement patterns / public appeals pathways / sanctioned-region interpretation); (b) suspension-cause diagnostics (spam / abuse / sanctions mis-flag / 2FA loss / ToS violation / account hijack false-positive / ban evasion taxonomy + symptom→cause mapping + self-triage pressure test); (c) appeal craft (writing an English appeal letter that gets T&S to fast-track a false-positive ruling + escalation ladder + legal-engagement boundary + Don'ts); (d) sanctions and compliance hard boundaries (OFAC SDN list / which countries/regions are truly unreachable / VPN false-positive paths / China is NOT a US-sanctioned region — repeated emphasis); (e) mainland China developer special circumstances (sanctioned-region mis-flag + SMS verification does not support +86/+852 + Gitee/GitLab/Codeberg/JihuLab fallback migration + self-hosted Gitea/Forgejo); (f) real-case library (~30+ public cases with categorized analysis: false-positive / genuine violation / sanctions mis-flag, each with win-rate and appeal success path). Ethical anchors absolutely not softened: do NOT teach ban evasion (creating new accounts turns a temp ban into a permanent one — the single biggest mistake); do NOT teach social engineering or lying in appeals (misuse + actually confirms violation); genuine violations: admit + improve beats denial; lost 2FA + no recovery factor → do not pretend recovery is possible; China sanctioned-region mis-flag → fix via appeal, not repeated VPN switching; appeal form 6-month window must be counted carefully; historical ~3% genuine-violation recovery vs ~79% false-positive appeal success rate stratification must be honestly labeled. Anti-samples (explicitly describe harm, never endorse): developer permabanned for ban evasion / open-source team org-banned for personal attacks in public issues / cases where repeated VPN switching escalated risk-control severity.
industry_cn: GitHub 解封 — 账号封禁/限制/恢复的领域操作系统。覆盖：(a) GitHub 官方政策体系 (TOS / 可接受使用政策 / Trust & Safety 执行模式 / 公开申诉路径 / 受制裁地区解读)；(b) 封号原因诊断学 (spam / abuse / 制裁误标 / 2FA 丢失 / ToS 违规 / 账号劫持误判 / ban evasion 等子类型 + 症状→原因映射 + 自我分诊压测)；(c) 申诉实操 craft (写一封能让 T&S 快速判误伤的英文申诉信 + 升级阶梯 + 法务介入边界 + Don'ts)；(d) 制裁与合规硬边界 (OFAC SDN 名单 / 哪些国家或地区真不可达 / VPN 误判路径 / 中国大陆不是美国制裁地区的反复重申)；(e) 中国大陆开发者特别处境 (sanctioned region 误标 + SMS 验证不支持 +86/+852 + Gitee/GitLab/Codeberg/JihuLab 备用迁移 + 私有自托管 Gitea/Forgejo)；(f) 真实案例库 (~30+ 公开案例分类拆解：误伤 / 真违规 / 制裁误标三类各自赔率与申诉成功路径)。伦理锚绝不软化：不教 ban evasion / 不教撒谎式申诉 / 真违规承认改进 / 丢 2FA 无恢复因子则诚实告知 / 中国制裁误标靠申诉不靠换 VPN / 申诉 6 个月窗口必算清 / ~3% 真违规恢复 vs ~79% 误伤申诉成功率分层标。
title: GitHub 解封

top_figures_for_sub_skills.name: Nat Friedman
top_figures_for_sub_skills.role: Former CEO, GitHub (2018-2021); drove key policy shifts including open-sourcing GitHub CLI and npm acquisition
top_figures_for_sub_skills.why: GitHub CEO during the period when Iran/Crimea sanctions enforcement was most aggressive + personally responded to community backlash on sanctions + set tone for modern T&S policy

top_figures_for_sub_skills.name: Nadia Eghbal (Asparouhova)
top_figures_for_sub_skills.role: Author 'Working in Public'; former GitHub open-source program; platform governance scholar
top_figures_for_sub_skills.why: Most systematic thinker on open-source governance + platform power dynamics + GitHub's dual role as infrastructure and content moderator

top_figures_for_sub_skills.name: Cory Doctorow
top_figures_for_sub_skills.role: Author, EFF advisor, platform power critic; coined 'enshittification'
top_figures_for_sub_skills.why: Most vocal and analytical critic of platform lock-in and account-banning power dynamics + prolific long-form output (Pluralistic blog daily posts) + directly relevant to understanding why platform appeals processes are structurally unfair

triggers: 我的 github 账号被限制了
warnings: locale=global: 官方政策文件和社区讨论 ~80% 英文 (GitHub 官方文档 / TOS / 博客 / HN 讨论 / 开发者论坛), 中国大陆开发者困境 (sanctioned region 误标 / +86 SMS / 迁移路径) ~15% 中文, 其他制裁地区 (伊朗 / 克里米亚 / 古巴) ~5% 英文。在 SKILL.md §8 诚实边界标明。
这是一个高度情景化的领域: 每个封号 case 的原因和最优路径不同, SKILL.md 提供的是诊断框架和申诉模板, 不是保证解封的 magic formula。必须在诚实边界反复强调 '不保证结果'。

伦理锚绝不软化: 不教 ban evasion (开新号把临时封硬化成永封, 第一大忌) / 不教社会工程或撒谎式申诉 / 真违规承认+改进 > 抵赖 / 丢 2FA + 无 recovery factor 诚实告知不可恢复 / 中国 sanctioned region 误标靠申诉不靠反复换 VPN / 申诉 6 个月窗口算清 / ~3% 真违规恢复 vs ~79% 误伤成功的赔率分层诚实标。反面样本明写危害绝不背书。

Phase 3 必跑 Step 3 sub-skill 生成 (用户 description 明确要求, skip_sub_skills=false) — 3 个并行 nuwa subagent 蒸 Nat Friedman / Nadia Eghbal(Asparouhova) / Cory Doctorow, 落 output/sub-skills/<figure-slug>/SKILL.md。H1 用「# <人物名> (<角色>) 视角 · Sub-skill」格式 (sync 的 syncSubSkills 只读 output/ + extractPersonaName 抽「视角」前缀)。
ethical_anchors: NEVER teach ban evasion — creating a new account turns a temporary suspension into a permanent ban; this is the single most common and most destructive mistake. Explicitly label this as the #1 anti-pattern.
NEVER teach social engineering or lying in appeals — misuse risks confirming the violation; GitHub T&S can verify claims against internal logs.
For genuine violations: admit + improve beats denial — the ~3% recovery rate for genuine violations mostly comes from demonstrated remediation, not from clever wording.
Lost 2FA + no recovery codes/SSH keys/PAT → do NOT pretend recovery is possible. Acknowledge the loss honestly and guide toward data recovery via GitHub Support data export request.

China is NOT a US-sanctioned region — sanctioned-region mis-flags for mainland China developers are almost always VPN-related IP geolocation issues, not actual sanctions. Fix via appeal explaining actual location, not via repeated VPN switching (which escalates risk flags).

Appeal form 6-month window: support.github.com/contact/reinstatement has a 6-month deadline from suspension. Past 6 months, the standard reinstatement path closes. Must count carefully and act promptly.

Stratified win-rate honesty: ~79% appeal success rate for false-positive/mis-flag cases vs ~3% for genuine ToS violations (based on aggregated community-reported outcomes, not official GitHub statistics — caveat this). Do not flatten into a single 'success rate'.

Anti-samples must describe harm explicitly and never endorse: developer permabanned for ban evasion / open-source team org-banned for personal attacks in public issues / VPN-switching escalation cases.
DMCA takedown vs account suspension are different enforcement tracks — do not conflate. DMCA has counter-notice provisions under 17 U.S.C. §512(g); account suspension does not.
GitHub is a private platform with no legal obligation to host anyone — appeals are a courtesy process, not a right. Set realistic expectations.

preemptive_surrogate_whitelist.comment: Phase 1 subagent prompts MUST declare these as surrogate_primary + literal 'vendor docs' in note. source_verifier judges most of these as secondary/unknown (not .gov/.edu/.org primary suffixes). Aggressive pre-emptive listing based on lessons from cybersecurity-red-team/ceo/dentistry-implant.
blacklist_reminder: 微信公众号 (mp.weixin.qq.com — 即使作者本人也 fail item 14; 用作者其他正版渠道替代)
CSDN (csdn.net) 除非作者本人原文
Medium SEO 农场 (通用路径 + 内容农场; 具体作者主页需谨慎)
Gartner / Forrester / G2 / Capterra / TrustRadius 主域 (分析师站含 UGC/PPC, blacklisted)
Reddit 帖子 (reddit.com — auto=reference, 不要标 verified_primary 或 secondary)

candidate_figures_for_track_01.comment: Track 01 subagent uses this list as seed. Final retention follows 2/3/4 rule. Diverse archetypes (GitHub insider + open-source governance + platform critic + China dev community) 中外兼顾.
github_insiders: Nat Friedman (前 GitHub CEO 2018-2021, 制裁期间决策)
Thomas Dohmke (现任 GitHub CEO, 公开谈 trust & safety policy)
Mike Hanley (GitHub CSO, Trust & Safety 政策发声)
Tom Preston-Werner (GitHub 共创, 早期社区治理)
Devon Zuegel (前 GitHub policy lead, 后 Astral, 长文输出)
Julio Avalos (前 GitHub CBO/CLO, 制裁合规决策)
open_source_governance_and_platform_crit: Nadia Eghbal / Asparouhova (Working in Public, 开源治理学者, 前 GitHub)
Mikeal Rogers (Apache & Node Foundation 治理, request/CouchDB author)
Cory Doctorow (EFF advisor, 'enshittification' coiner, 平台权力批评家, Pluralistic blog)
Lawrence Lessig (Stanford/Harvard, 'Code is Law', Creative Commons 创始人)
Daphne Keller (Stanford CIS, 平台中介责任 scholar)
Mishi Choudhary (SFLC attorney, 开源法律)
Bradley M. Kuhn (Software Freedom Conservancy, GPL compliance)
Karen Sandler (GNOME Foundation ED, SFC counsel)
Heather Meeker (开源许可 attorney, O'Reilly books)
sanctions_and_trade_compliance_experts: Erich Ferrari (Ferrari & Associates, OFAC sanctions attorney, prolific YouTube/blog)
china_dev_community: 庄表伟 Zhuang Biaowei (开源社理事, GitHub 中国生态倡导)
适兕 Shi Yi (开源社创始, 开源之道主笔, 开源治理研究者)
卫剑钒 Wei Jianfan (开源治理研究者, 开源社区观察)
anti_samples_in_canon_not_role_models: ban evasion 永封开发者 (匿名 aggregate, 不特指个人)
公开 issue 区人身攻击导致全组织封禁的开源团队 (匿名 aggregate)
VPN 反复切换导致风控加重的中国开发者 (匿名 aggregate)
youtube-dl DMCA takedown (2020, GitHub 后撤回 — 平台政策反转经典案例, 教育性但不鼓励)
Tornado Cash GitHub org removal (2022, OFAC-driven — 制裁执行对开源的极端影响, 教育性)
